nodes.name: bonato_onset_detection
nodes.description: Detects activity onsets in a signal using the Bonato double-threshold algorithm. It identifies points where the signal exceeds a defined threshold for a minimum duration.

inputs.name: signal
inputs.constraints: Input signal trace as a 1D numpy array.

inputs.name: rest
inputs.constraints: Signal segment corresponding to a rest period, used for noise estimation.

inputs.name: sampling_rate
inputs.constraints: The signal's sampling frequency in Hz.

inputs.name: threshold
inputs.constraints: The amplitude threshold for marking a potential onset.

inputs.name: active_state_duration
inputs.constraints: The minimum duration (in seconds) an active state must be maintained to be confirmed.

inputs.name: samples_above_fail
inputs.constraints: Number of consecutive samples that must be above the threshold to initiate an active state.

inputs.name: fail_size
inputs.constraints: Size of the window (in samples) to check for validation after a potential onset.

outputs.name: onsets
outputs.constraints: A list of integer indices corresponding to the detected onset locations in the signal.

nodes.type_signature: (signal: np.ndarray, rest: np.ndarray, sampling_rate: float, threshold: float, active_state_duration: float, samples_above_fail: int, fail_size: int) -> List[int]
nodes.conceptual_summary: persistent_threshold_event_detector. This algorithm identifies indices within a 1D numerical sequence that signify the start of a sustained state transition. It functions as a dual-criteria filter, first detecting when the sequence's magnitude exceeds a specified amplitude threshold, and then validating this transition only if the magnitude remains elevated for a minimum required duration. This distinguishes meaningful, persistent events from transient, noisy fluctuations.. Applications: Geophysics: Detecting the onset of seismic tremors in seismograph data, distinguishing them from transient ground noise by requiring the signal to remain above a threshold for a minimum duration., Finance: Identifying the start of a sustained period of high market volatility from a price time-series, filtering out insignificant single-tick price jumps., Manufacturing: Pinpointing the beginning of a machine malfunction (e.g., excessive vibration, temperature increase) from sensor data, ignoring brief, benign fluctuations., Network Security: Detecting the start of a persistent denial-of-service attack by analyzing incoming packet rates, differentiating it from a short-lived, coincidental traffic spike.

nodes.name: calculate_rest_variance
nodes.description: Calculates the signal variance during a specified rest period. The rest period can be given as a signal segment, start/end indices, or pre-calculated statistics.

inputs.name: signal
inputs.constraints: The full signal array, used if 'rest' provides indices.

outputs.name: rest_variance
outputs.constraints: The variance of the signal during the rest period.

nodes.type_signature: (signal: np.ndarray, rest: Union[np.ndarray, list, dict]) -> float

nodes.name: center_signal_at_zero
nodes.description: Removes the DC offset from the signal by subtracting its mean value.

outputs.name: centered_signal
outputs.constraints: The signal with its mean subtracted.

nodes.name: detect_events_with_state_machine
nodes.description: Calculates a test function over the signal and uses a state machine with dual thresholds and duration criteria to detect onset and offset event times.

inputs.name: rest_variance
inputs.constraints: Variance of the rest signal, used to normalize the test function.

inputs.name: threshold
inputs.constraints: The threshold for the test function to trigger a state change.

inputs.name: active_state_duration
inputs.constraints: Minimum duration (in samples) for a state to be considered stable.

inputs.name: samples_above_fail
inputs.constraints: Tolerable number of samples crossing the threshold during an opposing state.

inputs.name: fail_size
inputs.constraints: The window size to check for 'samples_above_fail'.

outputs.name: onset_times
outputs.constraints: List of detected onset time indices.

outputs.name: offset_times
outputs.constraints: List of detected offset time indices.

outputs.name: test_function_values
outputs.constraints: The calculated test function for each step.

nodes.type_signature: (centered_signal: np.ndarray, rest_variance: float, threshold: float, active_state_duration: float, samples_above_fail: int, fail_size: int) -> tuple[List[int], List[int], List[float]]

nodes.name: finalize_onsets
nodes.description: Merges the separate onset and offset time lists into a single, sorted array of event indices and scales them to match the original signal's sampling.

inputs.name: onset_times
inputs.constraints: List of detected onset time indices.

inputs.name: offset_times
inputs.constraints: List of detected offset time indices.

outputs.name: onsets
outputs.constraints: A sorted array of all event indices, scaled correctly.
